params.name: Churchill's Cigar
params.description:
On Oct 1, 1944, Churchill kept smoking cigars, one after another.

Churchill was thinking about the war while smoking. From the Italian front lines, he looked towards the enemy troops and soon many smoked cigars were scattered around the floor. As his servant said, "The number of cigars thrown away every day is equal to a week of my salary."

[A Record of Cigars Discarded]

April: 250. June: 275. July:100. August: 100. September: 100.
